questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?

Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed

Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What must you do at a steady red traffic light?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop and wait for green
Stop only if other cars are present
Yield to traffic on the left and continue
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires a full stop behind the stop line until the light turns green.

questions.prompt: A steady yellow traffic light means:
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red, so stop if you safely can
The same as a green light
questions.explanation: Yellow warns that red is coming, so you should stop unless you are too close to do so safely.

questions.prompt: What should you do at a flashing red light?
choices: Treat it like a stop sign: stop, yield, then proceed when safe
Slow down but do not stop
Stop and wait for it to turn green
questions.explanation: A flashing red light is treated exactly like a stop sign.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
questions.explanation: A flashing yellow light means reduce speed and proceed carefully.

questions.prompt: In Louisiana, when may you turn right at a steady red light?
choices: Only when a sign permits it
After a full stop and yielding, unless a sign prohibits it
questions.explanation: Right turn on red is allowed after a complete stop and yielding unless posted otherwise.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow center line on your side of the road mean?
choices: You may pass when it is clear
Passing is not allowed across that line
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means you must not cross it to pass.

questions.prompt: An octagonal (eight-sided) red sign always means:
questions.explanation: The octagon shape is reserved exclusively for stop signs.

questions.prompt: What color are signs that warn of road construction and work zones?
questions.explanation: Orange signs and markings indicate construction and maintenance work zones.

questions.prompt: A green arrow signal at an intersection means:
choices: You have a protected turn in the arrow's direction
Yield to oncoming traffic before turning
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives a protected movement in the direction it points.

questions.prompt: A yellow diamond-shaped sign generally indicates:
choices: A general warning of conditions ahead
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond signs warn of hazards or changing conditions ahead.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who proceeds first when two vehicles arrive at the same time?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop together, the one on the left yields to the one on the right.

questions.prompt: When making a left turn, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians in your path
No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles and crossing pedestrians.

questions.prompt: An emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren. What should you do?
choices: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull over to the right and stop until it passes
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right and stopping.

questions.prompt: A driver entering a road from a private driveway must:
choices: Yield to traffic already on the road
Proceed because they have the right-of-way
Stop only if a sign requires it
questions.explanation: Vehicles entering from driveways or alleys must yield to traffic already on the roadway.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you should:
choices: Stop and wait for all traffic to clear
Yield to traffic already circulating and enter on a safe gap
questions.explanation: Traffic already in the roundabout has the right-of-way, so you must yield.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians in a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: From which lane should you make a left turn on a two-way road?
choices: The lane nearest the center line
questions.explanation: Left turns are made from the lane closest to the center line.

questions.prompt: When should you signal before making a turn?
choices: Only if other cars are nearby
Well before the turn so others can react
questions.explanation: Signaling early gives other drivers and pedestrians time to respond.

questions.prompt: At an uncontrolled intersection where two cars arrive at the same time, you should:
choices: Yield to the vehicle on your right
Yield to the vehicle on your left
Stop and wait for a signal
questions.explanation: The general rule is to yield to the driver on your right when arriving together.

questions.prompt: A right turn should be made from which lane?
choices: The rightmost lane close to the curb
questions.explanation: Right turns are made from the rightmost lane, staying close to the curb.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit in a Louisiana residential or urban district?
questions.explanation: The default urban and residential speed limit in Louisiana is 25 mph unless posted otherwise.

questions.prompt: What following technique helps you keep a safe distance behind the vehicle ahead?
questions.explanation: The three-second rule gives a safe following gap under normal conditions.

questions.prompt: In rain or fog, your following distance should be:
choices: Increased to four seconds or more
Eliminated to keep up with traffic
questions.explanation: Reduced traction and visibility require a longer following distance.

questions.prompt: What is the maximum speed limit on most Louisiana interstate highways unless posted otherwise?
questions.explanation: Louisiana interstates generally allow up to 70 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: Even when below the posted limit, you must drive:
choices: At a speed reasonable for current conditions
At exactly the speed limit at all times
Faster on wet roads to maintain control
questions.explanation: The basic speed law requires adjusting speed to weather, traffic, and road conditions.

questions.prompt: What is a common school zone speed limit in Louisiana when children are present?
questions.explanation: School zones are commonly 20 mph when children are present or lights flash.

questions.prompt: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic is dangerous because it:
choices: Blocks traffic flow and can cause crashes
questions.explanation: Going too slowly obstructs traffic and increases the risk of collisions.

questions.prompt: Before entering a curve, you should:
choices: Brake hard in the middle of the curve
questions.explanation: Reducing speed before a curve helps you keep control through it.

questions.prompt: What is the default speed limit on a rural two-lane Louisiana highway unless posted otherwise?
questions.explanation: Rural two-lane highways in Louisiana default to 55 mph unless posted otherwise.

questions.prompt: How do you apply the three-second following rule?
choices: Count three seconds after the car ahead passes a fixed point
Stay three car lengths back at any speed
Allow three seconds to react after braking
questions.explanation: You count three seconds from when the vehicle ahead passes a fixed object until you reach it.

questions.prompt: When is it unsafe and illegal to pass another vehicle?
choices: On a hill or curve where you cannot see ahead
When the other car is slow
questions.explanation: You must never pass where limited sight distance makes it unsafe.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, the left lane is intended for:
questions.explanation: Keep right except to pass; the left lane is for passing and faster traffic.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder for blind spots
questions.explanation: Mirrors alone miss blind spots, so a head check is essential before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: When must you stop for a school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop arm?
choices: When traveling on the same roadway and not separated by a divided highway
questions.explanation: You must stop unless you are on the opposite side of a divided highway.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Seeing the vehicle in your mirror confirms you have enough room to return safely.

questions.prompt: While passing another vehicle, you may:
choices: Cross a solid double yellow line
questions.explanation: You must complete a pass without exceeding the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: The general rule for lane use on a multi-lane road is:
questions.explanation: Slower traffic keeps right and uses the left lane only for passing.

questions.prompt: Weaving in and out of traffic between lanes is:
questions.explanation: Weaving between lanes increases crash risk and is improper driving.

questions.prompt: You may not cross which line to pass on your side of the road?
choices: A solid yellow line on your side
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side prohibits passing.

questions.prompt: When another driver is passing you, you should:
choices: Maintain or slightly reduce your speed
questions.explanation: Holding steady or easing off lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist in Louisiana, you must leave a safe distance of at least:
questions.explanation: Louisiana law requires at least three feet of clearance when passing a bicyclist.

questions.prompt: Under Louisiana's Move Over law, when approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights, you should:
choices: Move over a lane if safe, or slow down if you cannot
questions.explanation: The Move Over law requires changing lanes when safe or slowing down significantly.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles harder to see than cars?
choices: They are smaller and fit in blind spots
questions.explanation: A motorcycle's small size makes it easy to miss, especially in blind spots.

questions.prompt: When driving near a large truck, you should avoid:
choices: Passing it on a straight road
questions.explanation: Trucks have large blind spots, so avoid staying where the driver cannot see you.

questions.prompt: How much following space should you give a motorcycle?
choices: A full lane and extra following distance
questions.explanation: Motorcycles deserve a full lane and a larger following gap for safety.

questions.prompt: When should you be especially alert for pedestrians?
choices: In school zones, parking lots, and residential areas
questions.explanation: Pedestrians are most common and unpredictable in these areas.

questions.prompt: Why should you not cut in front of a large truck and brake suddenly?
choices: Trucks need much longer to stop
questions.explanation: Large trucks require far greater stopping distance, so cutting in is dangerous.

questions.prompt: When you see a pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog, you should:
choices: Yield and give them the right-of-way
Expect them to yield to you
questions.explanation: A white cane or guide dog indicates a pedestrian who has the right-of-way.

questions.prompt: Large trucks making right turns may:
choices: Turn from the far left lane
Swing wide and need extra room
questions.explanation: Trucks often swing wide to turn, so give them space and do not squeeze beside them.

questions.prompt: Bicyclists on the roadway:
choices: Have no right to use the road
Have the same rights and duties as drivers
questions.explanation: Bicyclists are legitimate road users with the same general rights and responsibilities.

questions.prompt: In Louisiana, a driver age 21 or older is legally intoxicated at a BAC of:
questions.explanation: The legal BAC limit for drivers 21 and older is 0.08 percent.

questions.prompt: Under Louisiana's zero-tolerance law, a driver under 21 violates the law at a BAC of:
questions.explanation: Drivers under 21 are subject to a 0.02 percent zero-tolerance limit.

questions.prompt: The only reliable way to remove alcohol from your body is:
questions.explanation: Only time can lower your BAC; nothing speeds up the process.

questions.prompt: Refusing a chemical (breath or blood) test in Louisiana results in:
questions.explanation: Under the implied consent law, refusing a chemical test triggers an automatic suspension.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving in Louisiana is:
choices: Allowed if stopped at a light
questions.explanation: Louisiana bans texting and social media use while driving for everyone.

questions.prompt: Drivers with a learner's permit or under 18 in Louisiana are:
choices: Prohibited from using any cell phone while driving
questions.explanation: Learner's permit and under-18 drivers may not use any cell phone, including hands-free.

questions.prompt: Prescription and over-the-counter medications can:
choices: Impair your ability to drive safely
Only affect you the next day
questions.explanation: Many legal medications cause drowsiness or impairment that affects driving.

questions.prompt: The best way to avoid driving impaired is to:
choices: Plan ahead with a designated driver or ride
questions.explanation: Arranging a sober ride in advance is the safest choice.

questions.prompt: Using a handheld phone is specifically banned in Louisiana in:
choices: School zones and highway construction zones
questions.explanation: Handheld phone use is banned in school zones and active construction zones.

questions.prompt: Distracted driving includes:
choices: Taking your eyes, hands, or mind off the driving task
questions.explanation: Anything that diverts your attention from driving is a distraction.

questions.prompt: In Louisiana, your headlights must be on whenever:
choices: Your windshield wipers are in continuous use
questions.explanation: Louisiana's wiper law requires headlights on whenever wipers run continuously.

questions.prompt: In fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: High beams reflect off fog and reduce visibility, so use low beams.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and hold the wheel steady
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas without braking hard lets the tires regain contact.

questions.prompt: When is the road often most slippery?
choices: During the first rain after a dry spell
questions.explanation: Oil and dirt rise to the surface, making the first rain especially slick.

questions.prompt: If your car starts to skid, you should:
choices: Steer in the direction you want the front of the car to go
Turn the wheel opposite the skid hard
questions.explanation: Gentle steering in your intended direction helps recover from a skid.

questions.prompt: Which surface tends to freeze and become slick before others?
questions.explanation: Bridges lose heat on all sides and freeze before regular roads.

questions.prompt: When driving in heavy rain or flooding, you should:
questions.explanation: Even a few inches of water can sweep your vehicle away or stall it.

questions.prompt: On wet roads, you should:
choices: Reduce speed and increase following distance
questions.explanation: Wet roads lengthen stopping distance, so slow down and leave more room.

questions.prompt: Louisiana law requires headlights to be on:
choices: From sunset to sunrise and when visibility is poor
Only when other cars are present
questions.explanation: Headlights are required at night and whenever visibility is reduced.

questions.prompt: In foggy or rainy conditions, you should also:
choices: Slow down and increase following distance
Follow closely to see the car ahead
questions.explanation: Reduced visibility and traction call for lower speed and a larger gap.

questions.prompt: In Louisiana you may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: Parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant is prohibited.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning wheels toward the curb stops the car from rolling into traffic if the brakes fail.

questions.prompt: When may you park in a space reserved for people with disabilities?
choices: Only with a valid disability placard or plate
questions.explanation: Disability spaces require a valid placard or license plate.

questions.prompt: Seat belts in Louisiana are required for:
questions.explanation: Louisiana requires seat belts for the driver and every passenger.

questions.prompt: In Louisiana, children under 13 should generally ride:
choices: In the back seat when possible
questions.explanation: Children under 13 should ride in the back seat when seating is available.

questions.prompt: When parking uphill next to a curb, turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning away lets the rear wheel roll back against the curb if the car moves.

questions.prompt: Where is parking prohibited?
questions.explanation: You may not park on crosswalks, sidewalks, or within intersections.

questions.prompt: After parking on a hill, you should also:
questions.explanation: Always set the parking brake and leave the car in park or in gear.

questions.prompt: A young child who has outgrown a forward-facing harness should next use:
choices: The front seat with a shoulder belt
questions.explanation: A booster seat positions the adult belt correctly until the child fits it properly.

questions.prompt: When parking on a road with no curb, turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the center of the road
Toward the edge of the road
questions.explanation: With no curb, point the wheels toward the road's edge so the car rolls away from traffic.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Louisiana written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The Louisiana written knowledge test has 40 questions.

questions.prompt: What score is needed to pass the Louisiana knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the questions correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for a Louisiana Class E learner's permit?
questions.explanation: You may apply for a learner's permit at age 15 in Louisiana.

questions.prompt: A Louisiana learner's permit holder under 18 must be supervised by a licensed driver who is at least:
questions.explanation: Supervision requires a licensed driver at least 21 (or a licensed sibling at least 18).

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving must a parent certify before an intermediate license?
questions.explanation: At least 50 hours of supervised driving, including 15 nighttime hours, is required.

questions.prompt: An intermediate license holder may not drive between which hours unless accompanied by a licensed adult?
questions.explanation: Intermediate drivers may not drive from 11 p.m. to 5 a.m. without a licensed adult.

questions.prompt: A learner's permit must be held for at least how long before applying for an intermediate license?
questions.explanation: You must hold the learner's permit at least 180 days and be 16 before upgrading.

questions.prompt: Driver education for ages 15 to 17 in Louisiana includes how many hours of behind-the-wheel training?
questions.explanation: The course requires 30 classroom hours and 8 hours of behind-the-wheel training.

questions.prompt: Where must a supervising adult sit while a learner's permit holder drives?
choices: They need not be in the car
questions.explanation: The supervising licensed driver must sit in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: An intermediate license holder may carry how many non-family passengers under 21 between 6 p.m. and 5 a.m. without a licensed adult?
questions.explanation: Only one non-family passenger under 21 is allowed during those hours without an adult.

questions.prompt: What can result from a DWI conviction in Louisiana?
choices: License suspension, fines, and possible jail
No consequences for a first offense
questions.explanation: A DWI conviction carries suspension, fines, education, and possible jail time.

questions.prompt: Driving in Louisiana without proof of insurance can lead to:
questions.explanation: Driving without insurance can bring fines, impoundment, and other penalties.

questions.prompt: What is implied consent in Louisiana?
choices: Agreeing to chemical testing for impairment by driving
questions.explanation: Operating a vehicle means you consent to BAC testing when lawfully requested.

questions.prompt: Failing to move over for a stopped emergency vehicle in Louisiana results in:
choices: Fines that increase for repeat offenses
questions.explanation: Violating the Move Over law carries fines that grow with repeat offenses.

questions.prompt: What documents should you always carry while driving?
choices: License, registration, and proof of insurance
questions.explanation: You must carry and present your license, registration, and insurance proof when asked.

questions.prompt: Driving after your license has been suspended can result in:
choices: Additional fines and a longer suspension
questions.explanation: Driving on a suspended license adds penalties and extends the suspension.

questions.prompt: Reckless operation of a vehicle in Louisiana can lead to:
questions.explanation: Reckless driving is a serious offense bringing fines and possible suspension.

questions.prompt: Refusing a chemical test under implied consent leads to:
questions.explanation: Refusal triggers an automatic suspension separate from any DWI charge.

questions.prompt: Illegally passing a stopped school bus with flashing red lights can result in:
questions.explanation: Passing a stopped school bus is a serious violation with steep penalties.

questions.prompt: When a law enforcement officer requests your driver documents, you should:
choices: Present your license, registration, and insurance
questions.explanation: You are required to present these documents when an officer asks.
